Compute management engine in an artificial intelligence system Patent ID: 18-483307 Title: Compute management engine in an artificial intelligence system Abstract: Methods, systems, and computer storage media for providing compute management using a compute management engine in an artificial intelligence (AI) system. A compute management engine supports dynamically switching between two modes of operation for an inference phase of a generative artificial AI model. The compute management engine employs a bypass engine that causes prompt stage operations to be executed without an in-memory compute engine and causes auto-regression stage operations to be executed with the in-memory compute engine. In operation, an inference phase operation is accessed. When the inference phase operation is a prompt stage operation, the inference phase operation is executed without an in-memory compute engine. When the inference phase operation is an auto-regressive stage operation, the inference phase operation is executed with the in-memory compute engine. Memory output is generated for the inference phase operation to cause a processor to output a processor output for the inference phase operation. AI purpose: 인공 지능 시스템의 컴퓨팅 관리 엔진을 사용하여 컴퓨팅 관리를 제공하기 위한 시스템들, 방법들, 컴퓨터 저장 매체들을 제공한다. AI solution: 컴퓨터 엔진은 컴퓨터 모델에서 작업을 수행하는 두 가지 방법 사이를 전환할 수 있다. 메모리 엔진 없이 일을 할 때는 바이패스 엔진을 사용하고, 메모리 엔진으로 일을 할 때는 바이패스 엔진을 사용한다. 당신이 어떤 것을 할 때, 그것은 메모리 엔진을 필요로 하지 않습니다. 메모리 엔진으로 작업을 수행할 때 메모리 엔진으로 작업을 수행합니다. 메모리 엔진으로 작업을 수행할 때 메모리 엔진으로 작업을 수행합니다. 메모리 엔진으로 작업을 수행할 때 메모리 엔진으로 작업을 수행합니다. Representative claim: [청구항1] 1. A computerized system comprising:one or more computer processors; andcomputer memory storing computer-useable instructions that, when used by the one or more computer processors, cause the one or more computer processors to perform operations, the operations comprising:accessing a prompt stage operation associated with a generative artificial intelligence (AI) model, the generative AI model is associated with memory that supports executing operations associated with the generative AI model;based on the prompt stage operation, causing the prompt stage operation to bypass an in-memory compute engine of the memory;executing the prompt stage operation without using the in-memory compute engine;accessing an auto-regressive stage operation associated with the generative AI model;based on the auto-regressive stage operation, cause the auto-regressive stage operation to be executed using the in-memory compute engine of the memory; andexecuting the auto-regressive stage operation using the in-memory compute engine. System and method for hypergraph matching based on second and third order compatibilities with cur decomposition Patent ID: 18-497308 Title: System and method for hypergraph matching based on second and third order compatibilities with cur decomposition Abstract: Hypergraph matching algorithms have better matching accuracy at the cost of exponentially increased computation resources. Recent kd-tree-based approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) methods represent the compatibility between two hypergraphs as a sparse tensor, which still requires exhaustive calculation for large-scale graph matching problems. To decrease the computation for the large-scale hypergraph matching problem, this work proposes a CUR-based cascaded framework to generate the sparse compatibility tensor by decreasing the calculated compatibility between two graphs. The framework includes a CUR-based second-order graph matching algorithm, a fiber CUR-based tensor generation method, and a PRL-based hypergraph matching algorithm that is specifically suitable for sparse tensors. The experiment results show that the proposed cascaded framework requires much fewer computation resources and can effectively deal with larger-scale graph-matching problems. The PRL-based hypergraph matching algorithm can achieve a higher matching accuracy with a sparser tensor than existing methods. AI purpose: 2차 그래프 매칭을 위한 컴퓨터 시스템을 제공한다. AI solution: CUR 기반 종속 연결된 프레임워크는 두 그래프 사이의 계산된 호환성을 감소시킴으로써 희소 호환성 텐서를 생성합니다. 프레임워크는 CUR 기반 2차 그래프 매칭 알고리즘, 파이버 CUR 기반 텐서 생성 방법, 희소 텐서에 특히 적합한 PRL 기반 하이퍼그래프 매칭 알고리즘을 포함한다. Representative claim: [청구항1] 1. A computer system for second-order graph matching, said system comprising:a memory storing a representation of at least two set of nodes (1,2) and a compatibility matrix (H) representing similarities between edges of the nodes;a processing unit configured to receive the compatibility matrix and execute machine instructions comprising the steps of:retrieving a subset of columns and rows from the compatibility matrix (H) in the memory to compose a first matrix (C);conducting CUR decomposition of the tensor to generate an approximation matrix formed by the first matrix and a second matrix (U*); andapplying a soft-constraint second-order graph-matching algorithm on the approximation matrix to generate an assignment matrix (X);identifying a predetermined entries (k) with highest probabilities in the assignment matrix as match sets of the nodes (i). Memory device Patent ID: 18-587593 Title: Memory device Abstract: A memory device is provided, including a memory array and a selection circuit. At least one first faulty cell and at least one second faulty cell that are in the memory array store data corresponding to, respectively, first and second fields of a floating-point number. The selection circuit identifies the at least one first faulty cell and the at least one second faulty cell based on a priority of a cell replacement operation which indicates that a priority of the at least one first faulty cell is higher than that of the at least one second faulty cell. The selection circuit further outputs a fault address of the at least one first faulty cell to a redundancy analyzer circuit for replacing the at least one first faulty cell. AI purpose: 결함 셀을 대체하는 메모리 장치 및 방법을 제공한다. AI solution: 메모리 디바이스는 메모리 어레이 및 선택 회로를 갖는다. 선택 회로는 우선 순위에 기초하여 메모리 어레이에서 결함 셀들을 발견하고 대체한다. 그런 다음 오류 주소를 중복 분석기 회로로 전송하여 오류 셀을 교체합니다. Representative claim: [청구항1] 1. A memory device, comprising:a memory array comprising a plurality of memory cells;a selection circuit configured to identify a first faulty cell and a second faulty cell that are in the plurality of memory cells, wherein a first priority of the first faulty cell is higher than a second priority of the second faulty cell according to the first faulty cell storing an exponent field of a floating-point number; anda replacement circuit configured to perform a replacing operation to replace the first and second faulty cells according to the first and second priority. Machine learning architecture support for block sparsity Patent ID: 18-626599 Title: Machine learning architecture support for block sparsity Abstract: This disclosure relates matrix operation acceleration for different matrix sparsity patterns. A matrix operation accelerator may be designed to perform matrix operations more efficiently for a first matrix sparsity pattern rather than for a second matrix sparsity pattern. A matrix with the second sparsity pattern may be converted to a matrix with the first sparsity pattern and provided to the matrix operation accelerator. By rearranging the rows and/or columns of the matrix, the sparsity pattern of the matrix may be converted to a sparsity pattern that is suitable for computation with the matrix operation accelerator. AI purpose: 상이한 매트릭스 스파스 패턴들에 대한 매트릭스 동작 가속도를 제공한다. AI solution: 행렬 연산 가속기는 제2 행렬 성김 패턴보다는 제1 행렬 성김 패턴에 대해 보다 효율적으로 행렬 연산을 수행하도록 설계될 수 있다. 제2 성김 패턴을 갖는 행렬은 제1 성김 패턴을 갖는 행렬로 변환되어 행렬 연산 가속기로 제공될 수 있다. 행렬의 행 열들을 재배열함으로써, 행렬의 성김 패턴은 행렬 연산 가속기에 의한 계산에 적합한 성김 패턴으로 변환된다. Representative claim: [청구항21] 21. A method of making an executable neural network, the method comprising operations for:generating a first matrix having a first sparsity from a third matrix having a second sparsity by performing a transformation of the third matrix from the second sparsity to the first sparsity;generating a second matrix by performing a matrix operation in the executable neural network on the first matrix;performing a transformation of the second matrix to generate a result matrix; andoutputting the result matrix. Content summarization and/or recommendation apparatus and method Patent ID: 18-791020 Title: Content summarization and/or recommendation apparatus and method Abstract: Embodiments are provided for summarization and recommendation of content. In disclosed embodiments, a summarization engine scores constituent parts of content, and generates a plurality of summaries from a plurality of points of view for the content based at least in part on the scores of constituent parts. The summaries may be formed with constituent parts extracted from the contents. A recommendation engine provides recommendations to a user based on rankings of the summaries generated by the summarization engine. Other embodiments may be described and/or claimed. AI purpose: 복수의 관점들로부터 동적 요약을 제공하기 위해 온라인/오프라인 콘텐츠의 개선된 요약을 제공한다. AI solution: 요약 엔진은 콘텐츠의 구성 부분들을 스코어링하고, 구성 부분들의 스코어들에 적어도 부분적으로 기초하여 콘텐츠에 대한 복수의 시점들로부터 복수의 요약들을 생성한다. 요약은 컨텐츠로부터 추출된 구성 부분으로 구성될 수 있다. 추천 엔진은 요약 엔진에 의해 생성된 요약의 순위에 기초하여 사용자에게 추천을 제공한다. 다른 실시예가 청구된다. Computing device and method Patent ID: 18-817733 Title: Computing device and method Abstract: At least one embodiment provides a computing device including: a controller that receives first input data of a first data type and second input data of a second data type different from the first data type, and outputs a first signal representing the first data type, a second signal representing the second data type, and a clock signal based on the number of bits of the first input data and the second input data, and a computing circuit that performs a multiplication computation the first input data and the second input data based on the first signal, the second signal, and the clock signal and generates output data. AI purpose: 상이한 데이터 타입들을 갖는 입력 데이터에 대해 곱셈 및 누산 계산을 수행하도록 구성된 컴퓨팅 디바이스 및 방법을 제공한다. AI solution: 컴퓨팅 디바이스는 상이한 유형들의 데이터를 획득하고 얼마나 많은 비트들에 기초하여 신호들을 전송하는 제어기를 갖는다. 또한 신호와 클록 신호에 기초하여 데이터를 곱하여 출력 데이터를 생성하는 회로를 갖는다. Representative claim: [청구항1] 1. A computing device comprising:processing circuitry configured toreceive first input data of a first data type and second input data of a second data type different from the first data type,output a first signal representing the first data type, a second signal representing the second data type, and a clock signal based on a number of bits included in the first input data and the second input data, andperform a multiplication computation based on the first signal, the second signal, and the clock signal and generate output data such that a result of the multiplication computation is based on the first input data and the second input data. Techniques to utilize near memory compute circuitry for memory-bound workloads Patent ID: 18-834582 Title: Techniques to utilize near memory compute circuitry for memory-bound workloads Abstract: Examples include techniques to utilize near memory compute circuitry for memory-bound workloads. Examples include the near memory compute circuitry being resident on an input/output (I/O) arranged to couple with a plurality of memory devices configured as a memory pool that is accessible to a host central processing unit (CPU) through the I/O switch. The near memory compute circuitry may receive a request to obtain data from the memory pool and generate a result that is made available to the host CPU to facilitate acceleration of a memory-bound workload. AI purpose: 메모리-바인딩된 워크로드들에 대해 근거리 메모리 컴퓨팅 회로를 이용하는 기술들을 제공한다. AI solution: 예들은 메모리-바인딩된 워크로드들에 대해 근거리 메모리 컴퓨트 회로를 이용하는 기법들을 포함한다. 예들은 I/O 스위치를 통해 호스트 CPU에 액세스 가능한 메모리 풀로서 구성된 복수의 메모리 디바이스들과 결합하도록 배열된 입력/출력(I/O) 상에 상주하는 근거리 메모리 컴퓨팅 회로를 포함한다. 근거리 메모리 계산 회로는 메모리 풀로부터 데이터를 획득하기 위한 요청을 수신하고, 메모리-바인딩된 워크로드의 가속을 용이하게 하기 위해 호스트 CPU에 이용가능하게 되는 결과를 생성한다. Representative claim: [청구항26] 26. An apparatus comprising:circuitry resident on an input/output (I/O) switch, the I/O switch arranged to couple with a plurality of memory devices configured as a memory pool that is accessible to a host central processing unit (CPU) through the I/O switch, the circuitry to:receive a request from the host CPU to obtain data from the memory pool and process the data;obtain the data from the memory pool based on memory address information included in the request;process the data to generate a result; andcause the result to be stored to a memory accessible to the host CPU. Cluster covers PIM and near-memory AI hardware acceleration technologies for system integration and data management (AC/ACB). Patent 17-474372 (AMD, 2021), “Supporting processing-in-memory execution in a multiprocessing environment,” targets multiprocessing PIM offload control: processor logic receives a first-thread request to offload multiple PIM instructions to a PIM device and reserves PIM device resources for execution, emphasizing resource allocation, thread-issued requests, PIM instruction offload, multiprocessing coordination, and G06F-009/50. Patent 17-561406 (AMD, 2021), “Approach for providing indirect addressing in memory modules,” targets memory-module-level indirect addressing for PIM: processing logic in a memory module executes indirect PIM commands carrying address translation information, retrieves virtual addresses from chained memory locations, computes corresponding physical addresses, repeats pointer/indirection traversal until stop criteria, and cooperates with a memory controller; key terms include indirect PIM command, address translation, virtual address, physical address, memory module processing logic, and G06F-012/10. Patent 17-564155 (AMD, 2021), “Leveraging processing-in-memory (PIM) resources to expedite non-PIM instructions executed on a host,” targets acceleration of ordinary host memory operations using PIM resources: a memory controller identifies non-PIM write/read instructions, writes data into PIM registers via PIM commands, opportunistically moves data from PIM register to memory, or reads data directly from an associated PIM register; key terms include PIM register, memory controller, non-PIM instruction acceleration, read/write instruction, host execution, opportunistic movement, and G06F-012/0811. Patent 17-950560 (Cornell University, 2022), “Content-addressable processing engine,” targets programmable general-purpose PIM acceleration for vector operations: CAPE combines a control processor for scalar operations with compute-storage blocks (CSBs) that decode and execute vector instructions in situ through content-addressable memory operation sequences without ALUs, supporting RISC/RISC-V vector programming, associative computing, tiled multicore integration, vector acceleration, content-addressable memory, compute-storage block, and G06F-009/30.

Cluster focuses on processing-in-memory (PIM) and near-memory AI acceleration for data movement reduction, matrix/vector computation, convolution, and memory-side preprocessing. Patent 16-833864 (Seoul National University/Samsung Electronics, 2020) discloses a semiconductor memory device with multiple memory banks, including a first memory bank group having computation circuits and a second bank group without computation circuits, plus a control circuit that schedules or pauses PIM operations while processing memory requests under a maximum power consumption condition; target function is power-aware PIM execution in AI/neural-network-capable memory hardware; key terms include memory banks, computation circuit, control circuit, PIM operation, memory request processing, maximum power consumption, G06N-003/02, G11C. Patent 17-093538 (NeoNexus, 2020) covers matrix multiplication using PIM with an array of tiles, PIM blocks, memory units, peripheral logic, and a controller configurable between digital and analog modes; digital mode supports multiplication/accumulation and depth-wise convolution, while analog mode supports shift-add, partial sums, and point-wise convolution, targeting hybrid analog/digital PIM acceleration for neural-network matrix multiplication and convolution; key terms include tile array, hybrid-mode PIM block, memory array, memory units, peripheral logic, digital mode, analog mode, MAC, shift-add, partial sum, DW convolution, PW convolution, G06F-017/16. Patent 18-515565 (Seoul National University/Samsung Electronics, 2023) describes a multi-chip memory device where each chip has memory banks, memory cell arrays, word lines, row decoders, bit lines, sense amplifiers/write drivers, and a logic circuit that writes data based on host commands/addresses and performs first or second PIM operations using host-provided scalar/input data and stored vector data, including zero-check-oriented selective PIM execution; target function is host-command-driven memory-side vector/scalar AI processing with multiple operation modes; key terms include memory chips, memory banks, logic circuit, host command, host address, memory cell array, row decoder, sense amplifier, write driver, first/second operation mode, PIM operation, scalar data, vector data, zero check, G06F-012/02.

Patent 17-899531, "Memory device with embedded deep learning accelerator in multi-client environment" (Micron Technology, US, 2022), describes a memory device that integrates AI acceleration directly into memory for host-connected neural network computation. The apparatus includes at least one memory storing host data, a memory interface to the host, one or more embedded processing devices that perform computations for an AI/neural network model using stored data, and a controller that arbitrates memory access between the host and accelerator processing devices to avoid conflicts in a multi-client environment. The target AI hardware function is in-memory or near-memory deep learning acceleration, where neural network results are written back into memory and returned to the host over a DRAM/memory bus protocol. Key retrieval terms: embedded deep learning accelerator, memory device, AI model computation, neural network output, host device, memory interface, DRAM bus protocol, memory bus protocol, controller arbitration, memory access conflict avoidance, multi-client memory access, HBM/high-speed memory interface, G06F-013/42, G06F-013/32.

Cluster focus: high-bandwidth/high-capacity memory integration for AI hardware accelerators and memory-centric compute platforms. Patent 16-833043, "Multi-rank high bandwidth memory (hbm) memory" by Xilinx, targets implementation of multi-rank HBM in an electronic device. Core components include a first chip, interface circuit, first exterior pads, data input/output exterior pads, clock enable output exterior pads, HBM channels, data signals per channel, and clock enable signals per channel. The key architectural feature is that the number of clock enable output pads is scaled by a larger integer multiple than the number of HBM data I/O pads, enabling multi-rank HBM support through expanded rank/control signaling. Cluster covers memory-centric AI hardware patents focused on on-chip memory architecture, data reliability, and accelerator-near computation. Patent 16-984801, Samsung Electronics, "Memory device, and electronic device including the same," discloses a memory cell array with M memory cells connected to one bit line that distributively store N-bit data across cells having different sensing margins. A memory controller with a page buffer sequentially reads the distributed data using different read voltages for first and second memory cells, then supplies the reconstructed N-bit data to operation logic for neural-network/AI computation. Key retrieval terms: on-chip memory architecture, memory cell array, bit line, word line, page buffer, distributed N-bit storage, sensing margin, read voltage, sequential read, operation logic, neural network operation, memory-side computation, G11C-011/56, G06N-003/04. Patent 18-587593, National Tsing Hua University and TSMC, "Memory device," targets memory reliability for AI/numeric accelerator workloads by prioritizing faulty-cell replacement according to the semantic importance of floating-point fields. A memory array stores floating-point number fields, where faulty cells holding exponent fields receive higher replacement priority than faulty cells holding lower-priority fields; a selection circuit identifies first and second faulty cells and outputs fault addresses to a redundancy analyzer/replacement circuit. Key retrieval terms: memory array, faulty cell, cell replacement, redundancy analyzer circuit, replacement circuit, fault address, floating-point number, exponent field, priority-based repair, storage I/O optimization, platform management, accelerator data reliability, G06F-003/06.

Patent 18-362192 (TSMC, US, 2023) discloses a heterogeneous memory chiplet architecture and fabrication method for AI hardware memory integration. The die includes multiple memory regions formed on a single chiplet: a first SRAM memory array, a second 1T1C memory array, and a third FeFET memory array, with at least one of the 1T1C or FeFET arrays optionally implemented as a 3D memory array. A laterally extending data bus spans the SRAM, 1T1C, and FeFET regions to enable data transfer among the memory arrays, while peripheral circuit devices in a semiconductor material layer control the arrays. The target AI hardware function is dense on-die memory organization and inter-array data movement for chiplet-based accelerator or memory subsystem architectures. Key retrieval terms: memory chiplet, heterogeneous memory arrays, SRAM, 1T1C, FeFET, 3D memory array, lateral data bus, peripheral control circuits, die-level integration, chiplet interconnect, memory structure fabrication, AI accelerator memory.

Patent 16-405178, "Differential non-volatile memory cell for artificial neural network" by SanDisk, describes an on-chip non-volatile memory array architecture for AI neural-network hardware, especially BNN-style matrix multiplication and accumulation performed inside the memory array. The target AI hardware function is in-memory compute for neural-network inference/training operations: binary inputs are encoded as complementary voltage patterns on word line pairs, multiplied with stored synaptic weights, sensed on a shared bit line, and accumulated by summation circuitry. The core technical components are an array of non-volatile memory cells, word line pairs, a first/shared bit line, non-volatile memory cell pairs forming differential unit synapses, programmable-resistance memory cells, control circuits, sense amplifier, and summation circuit. Each unit synapse stores a neural-network weight using two individual memory cells connected between corresponding word lines and the shared bit line; applying high/low or low/high voltage levels to the word line pair represents binary input values and produces a bit-line voltage corresponding to multiplication of input and weight. Key memory and accelerator terms include non-volatile memory array, differential memory cell, unit synapse, programmable resistance, word line pair, shared bit line, voltage pattern, binary input, sense amplifier, summation circuit, matrix multiplication, accumulation, in-memory computation, neural-network architecture, hardware implementation of neural networks, static semiconductor memory, and on-chip memory architecture. Classification context: Middle class AC 시스템 통합 및 데이터 관리, small class ACA 온칩 메모리 아키텍처, main IPC G06N-003/04 neural-network architecture and topology, with related IPC/CPC covering neural-network hardware implementation, learning methods, and semiconductor/static memory technologies such as G11C-011/56, G11C-013/00, G11C-011/16, G06N-003/063, and G11C-016/04.

Patent 13-158002, "Non-volatile memory cell with BTBT programming," describes an on-chip non-volatile memory architecture for AI hardware memory/storage functions. The NVM cell stores at least two logic levels using a floating gate shared by a read transistor and a Band-To-Band-Tunneling (BTBT) injection device. When biased into accumulation, the BTBT device injects charge onto the floating gate to program a logic state. Key technical components include the floating gate charge-storage node, BTBT device with first node, read transistor with second node, read select switch connected to a bit line node, program select switch connected to a program select line, and programming/readout current paths. Patent ID 13-156702, titled "Nonvolatile semiconductor memory" (Toshiba/KIOXIA, US, application year 2011), describes a high-capacity nonvolatile semiconductor memory architecture relevant to AI hardware storage and memory-array functions. The device includes a semiconductor substrate, a first semiconductor layer, arrayed control gates passing vertically through the first semiconductor layer, data recording layers between the semiconductor layer and control gates, first and second conductive-type diffusion layers at opposing ends, select gate lines extending in a first direction, and word lines extending in a perpendicular second direction. The select gate lines act as shared select gates for select transistors connected between control gates and word lines, while each word line is commonly connected to control gates arranged in the second direction. The memory cell array forms NAND series with memory cells connected in series in the first direction. Patent IDs 18-047122 and 18-422386, both titled "Ai accelerator apparatus using in-memory compute chiplet devices for transformer workloads" and assigned to d-Matrix, describe an AI accelerator apparatus and operating method for transformer-based machine learning workloads. The architecture uses one or more in-memory compute chiplets on a substrate, where each chiplet includes multiple tiles, each tile includes multiple slices, a local CPU, and a hardware dispatch device. Each slice contains a digital in-memory compute (DIMC) device coupled to a clock and configured for high-throughput matrix computations with multiple multiply-accumulates per clock cycle. The DIMC devices accelerate transformer attention-function computation, while SIMD devices further process DIMC outputs and compute softmax functions. System integration components include die-to-die (D2D) interconnects, PCIe bus, DRAM interface, global CPU interface/global RISC interface, tile CPUs, host/server communication paths, and memory/processor data-transfer infrastructure. Patent 18-047122 emphasizes the apparatus structure: substrate, N chiplets, tiles, slices, CPUs, hardware dispatch devices, D2D interconnects, PCIe, DRAM interface, and global RISC/CPU interface. Patent 18-422386 emphasizes the operating method: a global CPU receives matrix inputs, distributes portions to tile CPUs over the global RISC interface, and DIMC devices execute matrix computations at high throughput. Target AI hardware function: accelerating transformer workloads, especially attention and softmax computation, using chiplet-based digital in-memory compute integrated with memory interfaces and accelerator dispatch/control logic. Key retrieval terms: AI accelerator, in-memory compute, digital in-memory compute, DIMC, chiplet, tile, slice, CPU, hardware dispatch device, SIMD, transformer, attention function, softmax, matrix computation, multiply accumulate, clock cycle, D2D interconnect, PCIe, DRAM interface, global CPU interface, global RISC interface, memory-processor data transfer, host system integration.

Patent 17-150891 (Samsung Electronics, US, 2021) describes an in-memory processing apparatus for AI hardware acceleration that reduces memory cell array load by converting column-current accumulation into delay/time-domain digital values. The architecture includes a memory cell array with memory cell groups receiving input signals on row lines and generating current sums of column currents on column lines, sampling resistors that receive sampling voltages corresponding to those current sums, voltage controlled delay circuits that output stop signals delayed from a start signal according to sampling-voltage magnitude, and a time-digital converter performing conversion at the delayed second time points. Target function: on-chip in-memory compute/processing for accelerator-style AI operations using current summation, voltage-controlled delay, and time-to-digital conversion. Key terms: in-memory processing, memory cell array, row lines, column lines, column currents, current sums, sampling voltages, sampling resistors, voltage controlled delay circuits, start signal, stop signals, delay dependent processing, time-digital converter, static/semiconductor memory devices, on-chip memory architecture.

Patent 16-224498, "Apparatuses and methods for memory device as a store for block program instructions" by Micron Technology, describes a memory-centric AI/accelerator architecture in which a memory device stores reusable blocks of program instructions locally in memory banks and executes logical operations using in-memory sensing circuitry. Patent ID 16-670461, "Updating a register in memory" by Micron Technology describes a processing-in-memory (PIM) memory apparatus for updating a register/reference inside memory to access stored data elements and perform logical operations directly on an array of memory cells. The system includes an array of memory cells storing multiple elements, a PIM controller coupled to the array, and a register storing a reference/address indicating a particular bit position of an element. The controller increments or decrements the reference by a value determined by element orientation: by the element bit-width in a first orientation or by 1 in a second orientation. The updated register reference is then used to access data for another element and execute logical operations in memory. Target AI hardware function: memory-side data access and logical computation for PIM/near-memory acceleration, reducing data movement between memory and processor. Key terms: processing in memory (PIM), memory cell array, controller, register update, reference/address, bit position, element orientation, increment/decrement, logical operation, in-memory computation, storage I/O integration, memory accelerator.

Cluster covers 3D-stacked AI processor and in-memory computing hardware for neural-network acceleration. Patent 17-994374, "Discrete three-dimensional processor," describes a discrete 3-D processor architecture for AI-oriented processing using communicatively coupled first and second dice. Patent 17-548220 (Samsung Electronics, US, 2021) describes data placement for in-memory compute (IMC) in a DRAM-based memory module to improve memory-module performance. The system includes a memory die with DRAM banks, DRAM cell arrays arranged into pages, row buffers, IO modules, IMC modules, ALUs, result registers, and a memory controller. The memory controller fetches image data from external memory, transfers it into input line buffers in FMM banks for feature-map extraction, and writes convoluted output data through output line buffers back to external memory. A flat dense computing unit converts feature maps into 1D vectors and performs dense multiplication. Key retrieval terms: reconfigurable CNN engine, 3D convolution engine, feature map extraction, parallel accelerator, kernel memory, network-layer parameters, memory controller, Input FMM cluster, Output FMM cluster, FMM banks, line buffers, external memory, dense computing unit, 1D vector conversion, dense multiplication, diagnostics registers, SEU fault, SET fault, AI image recognition hardware, neural-network accelerator, G06F-018/40, G06F-017/15, G06T-001/60, G06V-010/94, G06F-018/213, G06N-003/045.

Patent 18-246479, "Method and system for training a neural network" by Academy of Robotics, targets AI core/accelerator functionality for neural network training, especially image/video recognition using neural networks (G06V-010/82, G06V-010/778, G06V-020/56). The invention generates multiple sets of neural network weights, assigns or fits each weight set to a neural network, presents training data, and calculates a fitness score from a fitness function dependent on the network output. Patent 16-662981 (Alibaba Group / ALIBABA GROUP HOLDING LIMITED, US application year 2019) discloses a method and system for distributed neural network training in a neural network processing system using multiple heterogeneous computation units (HCUs). The target AI hardware function is coordinated neural-network learning and optimization across heterogeneous accelerators/processors. Each HCU computes first gradients from first training samples, the system aggregates these gradients into an aggregated gradient, then HCUs compute second gradients from second samples. At each HCU, the aggregated gradient is combined with the HCU-specific second gradient to generate a local gradient update, and each HCU updates its local copy of the neural network. Key technical components and retrieval terms include heterogeneous computation units, neural network processing system, distributed training, gradient aggregation, aggregated gradient, local gradient update, local neural network copy, sample-based gradient computation, accelerator coordination, heterogeneous system integration, neural-network learning methods, and optimization (IPC/CPC G06N-003/082, G06N-003/08).

Clustered patents cover AI accelerator chiplet/inter-die/interposer communication fabrics, high-bandwidth low-latency modular accelerators, stacked memory/processing systems, and in-memory compute for transformer workloads. Patent IDs 18-047122 and 18-422386, titled "Ai accelerator apparatus using in-memory compute chiplet devices for transformer workloads" and assigned to d-Matrix, describe an AI accelerator apparatus and operating method using one or more in-memory compute chiplets on a substrate. Each chiplet includes tiles, slices, local CPUs, and hardware dispatch devices; each slice contains a digital in-memory compute (DIMC) device coupled to a clock for high-throughput matrix computation with multiple multiply-accumulates per clock cycle. The target AI hardware function is accelerating transformer workloads, especially attention-function computation and softmax processing. DIMC devices accelerate matrix and attention computation, while SIMD devices process DIMC outputs and compute softmax functions. Patent 18-047122 emphasizes apparatus structure including substrate, N chiplets, tiles, slices, tile CPUs, hardware dispatch devices, D2D interconnects, PCIe bus, DRAM interface, global CPU interface, and global RISC interface. Patent 18-422386 emphasizes operation: a global CPU receives matrix inputs, distributes portions to tile CPUs over the global RISC interface, and DIMC devices execute high-throughput matrix computations. Patent cluster covers Amazon AI/ML accelerator synchronization and concurrency control for integrated-circuit execution engines. Patent 16-219610, "Synchronization of concurrent computation engines" (US, 2018), describes an AI accelerator or neural-network-capable IC with first and second concurrent execution engines, checkpoint registers, and synchronization logic. One instruction sets a checkpoint register after an engine completes an operation, while another instruction makes a second engine wait until a checkpoint-value condition is met. The checkpoint value encodes data or resource dependencies, and synchronization logic broadcasts the register value so waiting engines resume execution. Target function: synchronization of parallel computation engines in AI hardware. Key terms: concurrent execution engines, checkpoint registers, synchronization logic, register value broadcast, wait instruction, set instruction, data dependency, resource dependency, instruction execution, processor control, pipeline control, DMA, on-chip memory architecture, AI accelerator, neural network hardware, G06F-009/30, G06F-009/35, G06F-013/28, G06F-009/38, G06F-009/52, G06N-003/06. Patent 18-221640, "Vector clocks for highly concurrent execution engines" (Amazon, US, 2023), describes concurrency modeling and synchronization for AI accelerator/integrated-circuit execution engines using a computation graph with nodes and edges. Target function: highly concurrent scheduling, accelerator control, instruction execution, processor pipeline/control, and neural-network workload execution. Key terms: computation graph, nodes, edges, edge type, synchronization type, vector clock, connected preceding node, instruction phases, dependency, event registers, event register assignments, execution engines, integrated circuit device, AI hardware concurrency.
